question: How does the UV Index change throughout the observation times for each city?

analysis.design_rationale: A heatmap provides a compact grid view, mapping cities (rows) and observation timestamps (columns), with UV Index values represented by color intensity. Since both axes are categorical/discrete and the UV Index is low-cardinality, viewers can immediately spot patterns, correlations, and outlier UV Index values in a visually condensed matrix. Unusual temporal fluctuations or consistently high/low city-by-city UV exposure stand out in color rather than shape, which may reveal systemic or location-specific risks.
analysis.target_insight: This view exposes relative UV exposure as color blocks by city and time, revealing any city-wide temporal spikes/dips, and allows fast visual comparison across all cities and timestamps for UV Index extremes or patterns.

chart_encodings.color: UV Index (mapped to color intensity via colormap)
chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: Discrete color steps for UV Index; annotated values; colorbar for UV levels; grid and tick formatting for readability

transformations: Pivot data: rows are cities, columns are observation times, values are UV Index
Sort cities and times for visually logical heatmap ordering
data_preparation_steps: Create heatmap matrix with cities as rows and ordered times as columns, filling with UV Index values
suggestion.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib as mpl
df = pd.read_csv('csv_c_squared\243.csv')
df['Observation Time'] = pd.to_datetime(df['Observation Time'])
obs_times = np.sort(df['Observation Time'].unique())
locations = np.sort(df['Location'].unique())
pivot = df.pivot(index='Location', columns='Observation Time', values='UV Index').reindex(index=locations, columns=obs_times)
cmap = mpl.colormaps['inferno'].resampled(pivot.values.max())
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12,6))
im = ax.imshow(pivot.values, aspect='auto', cmap=cmap, interpolation='nearest')
ax.set_xticks(np.arange(len(obs_times)))
ax.set_xticklabels([t.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M') for t in obs_times], rotation=45, ha='right')
ax.set_yticks(np.arange(len(locations)))
ax.set_yticklabels(locations)
for i in range(pivot.shape[0]):
    for j in range(pivot.shape[1]):
        uv = pivot.values[i,j]
        ax.text(j, i, str(uv) if not np.isnan(uv) else '', ha='center', va='center', color='white' if uv >= 3 else 'black', fontsize=11)
cbar = plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax, fraction=0.03, pad=0.03)
cbar.set_label('UV Index')
plt.title('UV Index Across Cities and Times: Heatmap')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
